# Part 9 — Applications for a Financial Remedy

## PART 9 E+W APPLICATIONS FOR A FINANCIAL REMEDY


### CHAPTER 1 E+W APPLICATION AND INTERPRETATION


### [ F1 Application E+W


9.1. — (1) The rules in this Part apply to an application for a financial remedy.


(2) This Part is subject to any provision made by or pursuant to Part 41 (proceeding by electronic means).


(“Financial remedy” and “financial order” are defined in rule 2.3.) ]


Textual Amendments


F1 Rule 9.1 substituted (6.4.2020) by The Family Procedure (Amendment) Rules 2020 (S.I. 2020/135) , rules 1 , 9


### Application of Magistrates' Courts Rules 1981 E+W


F2 9.2. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .


Textual Amendments


F2 Rule 9.2 omitted (22.4.2014) by virtue of The Family Procedure (Amendment No. 2) Rules 2014 (S.I. 2014/667) , rules 1 , 7 (with rule 45 )


### Interpretation E+W


9.3. —(1) In this Part—


“ avoidance of disposition order ” means—

(a)

in proceedings under the 1973 Act, an order under section 37(2)(b) or (c) of that Act;

(b)

in proceedings under the 1984 Act, an order under section 23(2)(b) or 23(3) of that Act M1 ;

(c)

in proceedings under Schedule 5 to the 2004 Act, an order under paragraph 74(3) or (4); or

(d)

in proceedings under Schedule 7 to the 2004 Act, an order under paragraph 15(3) or (4);


“ the Board ” means the Board of the Pension Protection Fund;


[ F3 “fast-track procedure” means the procedure set out in Chapter 5; ]


“ FDR appointment ” means a Financial Dispute Resolution appointment in accordance with rule 9.17;
